summary: Deep-read the ~9 confirmed-real on-topic systems the wave-1 survey cut off on the session limit, + a synthesis that folds them into the 18 already read for the complete denominator.

per_system.system: MUSE / "Learning on the Job: An Experience-Driven Self-Evolving Agent for Long-Horizon Tasks" (arXiv 2510.08002; repo github.com/KnowledgeXLab/MUSE). Hierarchical experience memory — Strategic <Dilemma,Strategy>, Procedural SOPs, Tool memory — self-reflected from trajectories after each sub-task, evaluated on TAC/TheAgentCompany.
per_system.failure_mechanism: One-directional and within-episode only. Memory is written ONLY on success: in agent.py the update branch is gated `if cur_subtask.finish: if self.update_memory: ... memory_manager.update_and_save_app_memory(...)`. A failure produces a `failure cause analysis report Rfail` (paper) / `reflection.analysis` (code) that is injected into the RETRY of the SAME sub-task (temp raised to 1.5, guidance stripped) and logged for diagnostics — but it is NOT distilled into any durable, retrievable experience, and after the retry limit the path is sub-task replanning, not learning. DECISIVE for the driving question: when a RETRIEVED SOP leads to a bad outcome, NOTHING about that memory updates — no utility score, no trust score, no usage/frequency counter, no down-weighting, no prune. Verified absent in memory_manager.py (only deep_update + save_all_memory_to_disk; no scores/counters) and agent.py. The consolidation step is purely additive/dedup ('integrate new and old knowledge, eliminate redundancy, generalize common patterns') and never demotes/deletes on failure. So MUSE learns by ACCUMULATING success-experiences, and is PASSIVE on the retrieval-failure loop — the inert-utility-field / no-negative-feedback anti-pattern (letta/MemOS/A-Mem class) instantiated in a self-evolving agent.
per_system.outcome_signal: Sub-task success flag `cur_subtask.finish`, set by an LLM self-reflection completion check (reflect_check_completion_prompt) grounded in environment observations + execution traces; plus an OPTIONAL env_feedback_func hook that can inject TAC benchmark 'scoring points and score status' but defaults to None and returns empty string when absent. The memory-write gate depends on the LLM finish-check, not on the optional benchmark hook.
per_system.transferable_to_session_host: Yes — the default path is oracle-free. Success is judged by an LLM-as-judge completion check over trajectory + real environment feedback ('The only metric you use to determine completion is whether the associated goal (Goal) has been achieved'; 'Prioritize verifiable, real-world feedback ... rather than relying solely on the Task Agent's descriptions'), with tool/environment self-verification — no gold label, unit-test, or benchmark reward required. env_feedback_func is an optional ExpeL-style benchmark-oracle hook (TAC evaluator) but is None by default. Directly transferable to cmk: LLM-judge + tool-result self-verification as the outcome signal at conversation time. NOT transferable as a design goal is MUSE's memory model itself — it success-gates writes and has no utility/trust/prune on retrieval-linked failure, i.e. it is exactly the passive store-and-retrieve pattern cmk's trust_score dampen-on-contradiction is trying to move past; MUSE is a cautionary example, not a template, for the learn-from-failure loop.
per_system.novel_signal: none new — success-gated experience write + LLM-judge completion check + tool/environment self-verification, all already in the portfolio; no retrieval-utility signal exists at all (no counter, unlike ExpeL's survival-count-gates-prune)
per_system.quote: "if f=success, it summarizes the effective operational sequence from hk:t into a new SOP pnew for the Procedural Memory ... if f=failure, it generates a failure cause analysis report Rfail." / (code, agent.py) memory update runs only inside `if cur_subtask.finish: if self.update_memory: ... update_and_save_app_memory(...)`; completion set by `finish = extract_json_codeblock(finish_str)[0]; cur_subtask.finish = True if finish.get("finish","no")=="yes" else False`. / (reflect prompt) "The only metric you use to determine completion is whether the associated goal (Goal) has been achieved."

per_system.system: Evo-Memory / ReMem (arXiv 2511.20857, Google DeepMind + UIUC, Nov 2025). A streaming BENCHMARK + framework for "self-evolving memory": restructures 10 gold-labeled datasets (AIME, MMLU-Pro, GPQA, ToolBench, AlfWorld, BabyAI, ScienceWorld, PDDL, Jericho) into sequential task streams under a unified search->predict->evolve loop. ReMem is the proposed method: an action-Think-Refine MDP where Refine = LLM "meta-reasoning over memory (exploiting useful experiences, pruning noise, reorganizing M_t)." Retrieval is embedding-similarity Top-k via BAAI/bge-base-en-v1.5 (NO utility field feeds ranking). Memory entry m_t = h(x_t, y_hat_t, f_t) stores the step experience together with feedback f_t "such as whether the task was completed."
per_system.failure_mechanism: Learning-from-failure is the paper's whole thesis (test-time evolution: "learn from accumulated interactions"), and it is real but OUTCOME-ORACLE-GATED and SOFT. When a task's outcome is known, the experience is stored WITH its feedback f_t (success/fail flag). ReMem's Refine op then does LLM meta-reasoning over memory to prune noise / reorganize / "learn from successes while appropriately leveraging information from failures" (RQ4, Table 4 stores "both successful and failed task experiences"). BUT: (1) f_t IS the benchmark correctness signal ("Feedback is considered as the correctness signal") derived from the dataset's gold labels / exact-match / success-rate evaluator — a hard ground-truth oracle. (2) There is NO retrieval-utility field and utility does NOT feed ranking — ranking is pure bge embedding cosine similarity; a retrieved memory that led to a bad outcome does not get a numeric utility-down or ranking demotion. Failure handling is an LLM-judged prune/reorg inside Refine, not a mechanical outcome->rank feedback loop. The authors themselves flag this as open: "future work should explore failure-aware strategies for memory evolution."
per_system.outcome_signal: Benchmark task-success / correctness label f_t (gold-label exact-match for QA/math, success-rate + progress-rate for the AgentBoard multi-turn environments). Ground-truth, produced by the benchmark evaluator — not self-reported, not LLM-judged, not user-correction.
per_system.transferable_to_session_host: NO — hard oracle-gated. The entire signal f_t depends on knowing whether each task was completed correctly, which comes from the datasets' gold labels / environment success-rate at evaluation time. A cmk session host has NO reward oracle at conversation time (no unit-test pass, no gold answer, no env final-state), so the exact mechanism that makes ReMem "evolve" cannot fire. TRANSFERABLE IDEA (not signal): the Refine-as-LLM-meta-reasoning-prune (reorganize/dedup the store via a model pass) IS oracle-free and portable — but that is re-curation, not learning-from-failure. The failure-learning half needs an outcome label cmk does not have. Note the retrieval design is a cautionary mirror of cmk's own concern: embedding-similarity ranking with the outcome signal kept OUT of ranking (trust_score-as-partial-signal parallel) — ReMem confirms the field keeps outcome and ranking decoupled.
per_system.novel_signal: none new — the update signal is the benchmark task-success/correctness oracle (already in the "~12 need a benchmark oracle" bucket; not in the oracle-free portfolio). No new signal TYPE. One design NOTE worth carrying: ReMem's failure handling is an LLM-meta-reasoning Refine prune rather than a utility-field decrement, i.e. it AVOIDS the "inert utility field" anti-pattern by having no utility field at all — but it does so by pushing the judgment into an LLM pass gated on the oracle-derived f_t, so it is NOT an oracle-free learn-from-failure mechanism.
per_system.quote: "After obtaining [output], the agent constructs a new memory entry [m_t] that captures the current step's experience together with the feedback [f_t], such as whether the task was completed." — and, decisively, from the setup: "Feedback is considered as the correctness signal." (arXiv 2511.20857v1, §3.1 Evolve + §4.1.1). RQ4/§4.6: "selective utilization, which involves learning from successes while appropriately leveraging information from failures, is crucial for stable test-time adaptation ... future work should explore failure-aware strategies for memory evolution."

per_system.system: EvoTest (arXiv:2510.13220v2, ICLR 2026) — evolutionary test-time learning; evolves the whole agentic config (prompt + memory + hyperparams + tool-routines) after every Jericho episode via an Evolver Agent, config survival chosen by UCB over game-score fitness.
per_system.failure_mechanism: Explicit two-part Memory M: a success memory logging (state_hash, action)->score_delta AND a failure memory recording stall/no-progress patterns (repetitive loops). On a losing/stalled episode the Evolver does semantic credit assignment on the transcript: detects an unproductive loop (state AND score unchanged after an action), writes a failure entry, and DIRECTLY mutates the policy prompt with a verbal guardrail ('## Known Dead Ends / Wasted Actions — avoid west in <<hallway>>'), plus tunes hyperparams (raise temperature on loops) and rewrites tool-use routines. Across episodes, config statistics are updated from the episode return R(e) and UCB re-selects; a poorly-performing child config's average score drops so UCB falls back to the parent (score gates survival, ExpeL-class). So update surface = corrective/failure memory + prompt rewrite + hyperparam change + score-gated config down-selection. NOT passive store-and-retrieve.
per_system.outcome_signal: Jericho FrotzEnv game-engine scalar reward r_t = R(s_t,a_t); episode final return R(e) is the fitness. UCB performance term mu-hat(config) = 'the average score the configuration has achieved so far'. Ground-truth benchmark oracle. NO LLM-judge, NO self-report, NO rubric anywhere in the paper (grep judge/self-report = 0). Even the narrative loop-detection is score-anchored ('the state did not change, and the score did not change').
per_system.transferable_to_session_host: NO — load-bearing. What decides which evolved config survives across episodes (UCB fitness = average Jericho game score) and the score-half of the loop-detection both require the game-engine ground-truth reward, which a conversation host (cmk) does not have at capture time. Remove the Jericho score and both the fitness ranking and the failure-detection collapse. The ONE oracle-free crumb: the failure-detector's STATE-REPEAT half (same state_hash after an action = stuck) is oracle-free and mirrors cmk's recall-miss / used-vs-ignored family — but it is not sufficient alone; the paper pairs it with the score. Architecturally transferable idea (not a signal): evolve the WHOLE config unit (prompt+memory+routines) as one re-curation object rather than only the memory store — but its selection still needs an oracle cmk lacks.
per_system.novel_signal: none new — failure-memory-as-verbal-guardrail is a specialization of recall-miss/used-vs-ignored (no-progress loop) gated by an oracle; UCB-score-gates-survival is ExpeL-class count/score-survival (oracle-dependent).
per_system.quote: "Performance Term (μ̂(χ̃)): This is simply the average score the configuration has achieved so far. It encourages us to re-use configurations that have a good track record." — and the failure-detection: "Analysis: The state did not change, and the score did not change. This action is flagged as unproductive for this state."

per_system.system: SkillRL (arXiv 2602.08234, "Evolving Agents via Recursive Skill-Augmented Reinforcement Learning"; aiming-lab/SkillRL). LLM-agent RL framework: distills environment rollouts into a hierarchical SkillBank (general + task-specific skills), cold-start SFT, then GRPO with a recursive skill-evolution loop. Benchmarks: ALFWorld, WebShop, 7 search-augmented QA tasks.
per_system.failure_mechanism: Failure is first-class and drives THREE mechanisms. (1) Distillation: failed trajectories are NOT discarded (its whole pitch vs prior memory methods) but synthesized into "concise failure lessons" s- = M_T(tau-, d), analyzing "(1) the point of failure, (2) the flawed reasoning or action, (3) what should have been done, and (4) general principles to prevent similar failures ... transforms verbose failed episodes into counterfactuals." (2) Recursive skill evolution: after each validation epoch, for task categories where success rate rho_c < threshold tau, it collects failed validation trajectories via "diversity-aware stratified sampling ... prioritized by the severity of failure (negative rewards)", and the teacher "(1) identify[s] failure patterns not addressed by current skills, (2) propose[s] new skills to cover these gaps, and (3) suggest[s] refinements to existing skills that proved ineffective"; SkillBank <- SkillBank U S_new. (3) GRPO: failed trajectory (R=0) gets negative normalized advantage, down-weighting the policy that produced it. NOTE: skills are APPEND-ONLY (union) — ineffective skills are "refined", never pruned or down-ranked; no per-skill utility field. So policy learns from failure, but the skill store itself never removes a bad skill (same no-prune anti-pattern side as letta/MemOS, softened by refinement).
per_system.outcome_signal: Binary task-success reward r(tau) in {0,1} straight from the benchmark environment: ALFWorld task-completion, WebShop purchase-match score/success, and gold-answer EM for search QA (NQ/TriviaQA/PopQA/HotpotQA/2Wiki/MuSiQue/Bamboogle). "Each trajectory tau(i) receives a binary reward R_i = r(tau(i)) in {0,1} indicating task successfulness." Both the failure-lesson label AND the evolution trigger (success rate < threshold, severity = reward magnitude) AND the GRPO advantage are all derived from this one environment reward.
per_system.transferable_to_session_host: NO — hard oracle-gated end to end. Every failure signal (the {0,1} label that splits success/failure trajectories, the negative-reward "severity" that prioritizes which failures to learn from, the per-category success-rate that triggers skill evolution, and the GRPO advantage) is the benchmark environment's ground-truth task-success reward: ALFWorld completion, WebShop match, gold-answer EM. cmk has NO conversation-time binary success oracle, so none of SkillRL's three failure loops can run. What is CONCEPTUALLY (not mechanically) borrowable and oracle-free if re-grounded on a transferable signal: (a) the failure-lesson SHAPE — capture a corrective note structured as {point-of-failure, flawed action, what-should-have-been-done, general principle} instead of raw trajectory (cmk could emit this from a user-correction or tool-error, no oracle); (b) threshold-gated evolution (only revise memory for topics whose recent outcomes are bad). But SkillRL's own labels are 100% benchmark-oracle; treat the borrowable part as pattern-transfer, not signal-transfer.
per_system.novel_signal: severity-weighted-failure-sampling — evolution prioritizes failed trajectories by MAGNITUDE of negative reward, not just binary fail (round-robin across categories to keep entropy). Also threshold-gated evolution (evolve only categories with success-rate < tau, cousin of ExpeL's count-gates-survival). BOTH are oracle-derived here (severity = benchmark reward magnitude), so NOT a new oracle-free signal — but "prioritize learning by how-bad the failure was, not just whether it failed" is a signal-SHAPING idea worth noting if cmk ever gets a graded transferable outcome (e.g. user re-correcting the same fact twice = higher severity than once). Net: none new that is oracle-free.
per_system.quote: "Unlike prior approaches that retain only successful episodes, we deliberately preserve both successful trajectories and failed trajectories, where [r(tau)] denotes the binary task success indicator." / "For failed trajectories ... we synthesize concise failure lessons ... The analysis identifies: (1) the point of failure, (2) the flawed reasoning or action, (3) what should have been done, and (4) general principles to prevent similar failures. This transforms verbose failed episodes into counterfactuals." / "the evolution is triggered only for categories where [success rate < threshold]. We then collect failed trajectories using a diversity-aware stratified sampling strategy: trajectories are grouped by category, prioritized by the severity of failure (negative rewards) ... Each trajectory receives a binary reward indicating task successfulness."

per_system.system: SkillRevise (arXiv 2606.01139v3, HKUST-KnowComp) — trace-conditioned skill revision. Code: github.com/HKUST-KnowComp/skillrevise
per_system.failure_mechanism: Learning is WITHIN-EPISODE only and ORACLE-DRIVEN. Loop: execute skill -> Diagnosis D_i=(V_i,A_i,K_i) built from verifier-facing failures -> retrieve top-m repair principles from a (frozen) Principle Memory -> apply execution-anchored edit -> re-execute -> success-prioritized selection. Round transition e_i=(tau_i, v_i, r_i, c_i) where v_i is "verifier feedback" and r_i is "the outcome score or pass/fail reward". Selection (Eq.8): keep the first verifier-passing candidate (succ(S,T)=1); utility U(S,T) is used ONLY to choose among FAILED candidates when none pass — an inert-until-fallback utility, NOT a ranking/retrieval signal. Utility itself is oracle-gated: gsucc(S,T)=1[succ(S,T)=1], so efficiency credit only accrues after passing the verifier. CROSS-TASK: the Principle Memory (7 seed principles) is FROZEN by default — "online absorption is not used to improve later test tasks unless explicitly stated." So no memory-layer learning across tasks in the reported setup; the optional 'absorption' (dashed arrow, ALFWorld study only) is off by default AND gated on the same oracle (candidate principles must be 'utility-improving'). Net: failure updates the skill within an episode via a ground-truth test oracle; the durable memory that would carry learning forward does not update. No prune-at-zero, no cross-session utility ranking.
per_system.outcome_signal: Verifier pass/fail from a per-task test harness ("a verifier entrypoint under tests/", pass/fail assertions, output paths, schemas, terminal sentinels) + a success-conditioned empirical utility U(S,T) that only tie-breaks failed candidates.
per_system.transferable_to_session_host: NO — decisively oracle-gated. The verifier is literally task-specific tests copied from the benchmark bundle ("a verifier entrypoint under tests/"; r_i = "pass/fail reward"; selection = succ(S,T)=1). The ALFWorld variant swaps in environment final-state success ("admissible action syntax and state tracking"). Both are ground-truth oracles cmk does not have at conversation time. The paper's own Limitations concede it: "SKILLREVISE depends on verifier-visible feedback... Our evaluation is also limited to verifier-based benchmarks." The one transferable-shaped idea — trace-conditioned Diagnosis -> repair-principle retrieval from a general memory — is still anchored on verifier-FACING failures, so it inherits the oracle dependence. cmk could borrow the STRUCTURE (diagnose a bad recall from execution evidence, retrieve a repair principle) but would have to substitute an oracle-free trigger (user-correction / tool-result / contradiction), which the paper does not provide.
per_system.novel_signal: none new — verifier pass/fail is the benchmark-oracle signal already catalogued; the empirical utility is the inert-until-fallback anti-pattern (letta/MemOS/A-Mem family), used even more weakly here (fallback tie-breaker among failures, not a ranking field). Trace-conditioned diagnosis is an interesting mechanism but not a new SIGNAL type — it consumes verifier failure, not a novel oracle-free source.
per_system.quote: "SKILLREVISE depends on verifier-visible feedback and spends additional execution budget to obtain it. Sparse or misaligned tests can lead revision to overfit visible checks... Our evaluation is also limited to verifier-based benchmarks rather than long-running or safety-critical deployments." Corroborating: "the episode generates candidate revisions and returns the first evaluated skill that passes the verifier. If no evaluated skill succeeds within the budget, it returns a utility fallback." And on memory: "Reported benchmark results use the fixed Principle Memory available before evaluation; online absorption is not used to improve later test tasks unless explicitly stated."

per_system.system: SkillAdaptor (arXiv:2606.01311v1, Yu/Xie et al., Zhejiang U. + Ant Group; code promised at github.com/zjunlp/SkillAdaptor but NOT yet released — evidence is PAPER-only, no code to read).
per_system.failure_mechanism: Q1 (learns from failure?): YES — step-level credit assignment, the paper's whole thesis, not passive store-and-retrieve. A FAILED trajectory drives a 3-stage loop: (1) ATTRIBUTION — a Localizer predicts s*=Localize(tau,f), "the earliest accountable fault step whose correction is expected to improve the final outcome," plus f = "a description of observed failure behavior and improvement suggestion" (eq 5); a Linker then produces a WEIGHTED suspect set over the RETRIEVED skills — "a larger [weight] indicates stronger estimated responsibility for the failure" — and picks action a in {revise, generate} (eq 6). (2) MODIFICATION — if failure is attributed to "an inappropriate or misleading retrieved skill," the highest-weighted skill is REWRITTEN in place; else it is a "capability gap due to missing skill coverage" and a NEW skill is synthesized (eq 7). (3) QUALIFICATION — the edit is re-executed and accepted only if outcome improves (eq 8). So a retrieved memory that led to a bad outcome IS updated: the specific skill card's faulty span is rewritten (case study: PinchBench failure at interaction step six mapped to "the fourth line of the pre-revision skill"). This is exactly the retrieved-memory-caused-the-failure -> targeted-edit loop cmk cares about. Q2 (needs a ground-truth oracle?): YES — DECISIVELY oracle-gated at TWO points, though attribution itself is oracle-free. (a) ENTRY: the loop only fires on trajectories labeled failed by y = "the benchmark-defined success indicator" (eq 1-3); nothing enters attribution without the benchmark declaring failure. (b) SURVIVAL: Qualification (eq 8) accepts an edit only when 1[m(C')>m(C)], m = "the execution feedback metric" — it re-runs the benchmark and keeps the edit only if the SCORE rises. Appendix B.1: "the adaptor only has access to execution trajectories and their success or failure signals" — that signal is the benchmark grader's verdict (WebShop's "standard evaluator released by the benchmark authors"; PinchBench/Claw-Eval's benchmark-provided grading pipeline). Localizer/Linker are oracle-free LLM-judge, but only run on oracle-declared failures and every edit is discarded unless a fresh benchmark re-execution scores higher. Ablation proves the gate is load-bearing: Table 3 row (3) "w/o qualifier gate" collapses WebShop success 33.0->26.3 and raises variance — "removing qualification allows unstable skills to enter retrieval."
per_system.outcome_signal: Two-part: (1) attribution/localization = LLM-judge over the execution trace (Localizer + Linker; oracle-free; self-reported "which step/skill is responsible"); (2) entry-trigger AND edit-survival = benchmark success indicator y + re-execution metric m (ground-truth oracle). Learning is gated by (2) at both ends.
per_system.transferable_to_session_host: MIXED — one transferable idea inside an oracle-dependent shell. NOT transferable as a system: cmk has no benchmark success indicator y and cannot re-execute a conversation to score m(C')>m(C), so both the entry-trigger AND the qualifier gate — the two load-bearing oracle points (ablation: dropping the qualifier collapses gains) — are unavailable at conversation time. Same fatal dependency as the ~12 benchmark-oracle wave-1 systems. TRANSFERABLE part: the Localizer->Linker fine-grained credit assignment is oracle-free in isolation — an LLM-judge reads the trace and names (a) the earliest fault step and (b) a WEIGHTED responsibility distribution over the retrieved memories, then routes revise-vs-generate. cmk could adapt this WITHOUT the oracle: when the user corrects the assistant (user-correction, already in portfolio) or a tool result contradicts an injected fact, run an LLM-judge to attribute blame to the SPECIFIC injected memory (the Linker step) instead of flat-dampening the whole recalled set, then down-weight/rewrite only the culprit's trust_score. The capability cmk lacks today is the WEIGHTED per-memory blame split across the recalled set. But cmk MUST NOT copy the Qualification gate (eq 8) — that is the part needing the oracle cmk cannot build, and it is what makes the published numbers hold; harvest the blame-attribution mechanism, discard the acceptance-check.
per_system.novel_signal: weighted-per-memory-blame-attribution (the Linker's weighted responsibility distribution over the RETRIEVED set for a single failure — "which of the recalled memories caused this," a blame-SPLIT). Distinct from cmk's contradiction/used-vs-ignored (per-fact binary), from A-MemGuard's group-consensus (peer-disagreement across memories), and from graph-memory's counterfactual-deltaR (needs re-execution). This is single-trajectory, oracle-free credit assignment across the recalled set. Caveat: in SkillAdaptor it is yoked to a benchmark oracle for entry+survival, so its standalone reliability is unproven paper-side — but the attribution mechanism itself is oracle-free and not in cmk's current portfolio.
per_system.quote: "If the failure is attributed to an inappropriate or misleading retrieved skill, the trajectory is routed to skill revision. Otherwise, the failure is treated as an uncovered capability gap due to missing skill coverage and routed to new skill generation." (Sec 3, Attribution); the oracle tell: "The candidate update is accepted only when [m(C') > m(C)]. Otherwise, the modification is discarded and the original collection is retained unchanged." (Sec 3, Qualification, eq 8); confirmed by Appendix B.1: "the adaptor only has access to execution trajectories and their success or failure signals."

per_system.system: Negative Knowledge as Failure-aware Shared Memory for AutoResearch (arXiv 2606.21024, Hanchun Wang, ICML 2026 AI4Research Workshop; code: github.com/hch-wang/Negative_Knowledge). A curator agent converts each FAILED attempt into a bounded, TYPED JSON "negative-knowledge" (NK) record in a shared project-level bank; a downstream research agent must explicitly ADOPT or REJECT each relevant record (recording cites_bank / rejects_bank / bank_use_rationale on its Experiment node) before proposing its next experiment. Evaluated on same-task retry (ScienceAgentBench deterministic-eval, 38 tasks) and cross-task PDE transfer (BKdV / Burgers-NLS).
per_system.failure_mechanism: Learn-from-failure at the WRITE boundary only, passive at the RETRIEVAL/UTILITY boundary. The entire system is failure-centric: a separate curator turns each failed attempt into a typed record ("a curator agent converts each failed attempt into a bounded, typed record in a shared bank") — the closest wave-2 analog to cmk's auto-extract capturing a lesson from a bad turn. BUT there is NO write-back from OUTCOME to a PRIOR retrieved record. A record, once curated, is frozen: the schema (task_id, attempted_route, observation, failure{layer,scope,degree,recommended_action,risk}, rationale, recommended_alternative; depth-N adds rounds_summary/ruled_out_routes/synthesised_diagnosis) has NO utility/trust/score/count/timestamp field. Code confirms it: the whole public module surface is NKCurator (write), validate_nk (schema check), run_subagent (constrained Read/Write loop) — grep-equivalent shows zero rank/score/utility/prune/decay/reinforce functions and no retrieval scorer at all (the ENTIRE bank is passed inline: "an entire bank can be passed to an agent inline"). When a consulted record leads to ANOTHER failure, the loop produces a NEW record appended to the bank — it does NOT down-rank, dampen, or prune the prior record that was adopted, nor reinforce one that helped. The adopt/reject decision (cites_bank/rejects_bank) is written onto the reading agent's Experiment node, NOT back onto the bank record — so the record accrues no reputation. This is the field-wide "inert utility field" anti-pattern taken to its limit: not merely an unused utility field, but NO utility field and NO outcome->record feedback edge. It is one-shot re-curation on every retry, never trust_score-style reinforcement/dampening on contradiction/restatement.
per_system.outcome_signal: Two-tier. (a) SURVIVAL / success signal (what counts as a "failed attempt" worth curating, and whether a retry "passed") = a HARD GROUND-TRUTH ORACLE: ScienceAgentBench's deterministic programmatic evaluator ("outcomes are verified by programmatic evaluators"; "pass rate (% tasks passing the deterministic evaluator)") and, in the PDE case study, deterministic physics-aware gates (NaN-free, mass-drift < threshold, amplitude/peak-count checks). (b) CONTENT signal (the typed failure classification + recommended_alternative, and the per-proposal adopt/reject) = LLM/agent self-reported (curator agent writes the failure taxonomy from frozen artifacts; research agent writes bank_use_rationale). Nothing self-reported updates a memory's utility; the only thing that gates the pipeline is the benchmark oracle.
per_system.transferable_to_session_host: Partially — and the transferable half is the WRITE-time mechanism, not a retrieval-utility loop. TRANSFERABLE to cmk: (1) the curator-converts-failure-into-a-bounded-typed-record pattern maps cleanly onto auto-extract writing a lesson when a turn goes wrong — no oracle needed to WRITE the record, only to know a "failure" happened (in a session host, the failure signal is user-correction / tool-error / cmk-forget, which cmk already has). (2) The TYPED CLOSED-VOCABULARY failure schema (layer/scope/degree/recommended_action/risk with values like contradicted/unstable/overclaimed, abandon_route, high_risk_false_progress) is directly liftable as a richer fact-type taxonomy for negative/cautionary memories — structured "what not to do + why + what to do instead" beats a free-prose bullet. (3) The adopt/reject-with-justification grounding requirement (reading agent must explicitly mark each retrieved memory adopted or rejected) is a lightweight, oracle-free "used-vs-ignored" instrumentation cmk could adopt to make recall auditable. NOT transferable / NOT PRESENT: any utility-down / prune / ranking-change on a retrieved memory after a bad outcome — the system simply does not have this; and its pass/fail gate depends on a benchmark oracle cmk lacks at conversation time. Also note the whole bank is injected wholesale (no ranked retrieval), which does NOT scale to cmk's bounded-snapshot / BM25-select design.
per_system.novel_signal: route-closure / explicit-dead-end veto (recommended_action=abandon_route + depth-N ruled_out_routes + rejects_bank): a memory that functions as a NEGATIVE CONSTRAINT the reading agent must reject-AGAINST, distinct from a positive fact to adopt. This is a first-class "do-not-retry-this-route" signal type not in the known portfolio (contradiction is about two facts disagreeing; this is a standalone dead-end marker with a typed risk/degree). Secondary (not new as a mechanism but a novel PACKAGING): the typed failure-degree vocabulary (contradicted/partial/inconclusive/unstable/artifact_driven/overclaimed) elevates Memoria's "outdated-as-first-class" into a 6-way write-time failure taxonomy. Caveat: both are WRITE-TIME content signals, not post-retrieval utility signals — so neither closes the learn-from-failure loop at the ranking layer.
per_system.quote: "A curator agent converts each failed attempt into a bounded, typed record in a shared bank, and a downstream research agent explicitly adopts or rejects those records before proposing its next experiment." (Abstract) — and on the oracle: "All experiments in this section use Claude Sonnet 4.6 on the deterministic-evaluation subset of ScienceAgentBench, where outcomes are verified by programmatic evaluators. ... We report pass rate (% tasks passing the deterministic evaluator)." (§3). Schema confirms no utility field: "The base record contains task_id, attempted_route, observation, failure, rationale, and recommended_alternative. ... layer, scope, degree, action, and risk." (Appendix A) — with action ∈ {retry, change_method, narrow_claim, abandon_route} and risk ∈ {low_risk_omission, medium_risk_drift, high_risk_false_progress}.

per_system.system: AgentDebug — "Where LLM Agents Fail and How They can Learn From Failures" (arXiv 2509.25370, ulab-uiuc). A single-LLM agent failure-diagnosis + within-episode recovery framework; ships AgentErrorTaxonomy (17 error types / 5 modules: memory, reflection, planning, action, system) + AgentErrorBench (200 annotated failure trajectories from ALFWorld/GAIA/WebShop) + the AgentDebug 3-stage pipeline. NOT a memory store.
per_system.failure_mechanism: Within-episode ONLY — no cross-session/persistent learning. On a failed trajectory: Stage 1 assigns fine-grained per-step per-module error types; Stage 2 an LLM judge picks the EARLIEST root-cause step t* ('Critical Error Detection via LLM (no rollout/counterfactuals)', Alg.1); Stage 3 generates targeted feedback phi and RE-ROLLS OUT from t* on the SAME task instance, refining phi up to N=5 attempts. There is NO persistent memory update, NO utility/trust score, NO corrective-memory store, NO pruning, NO ranking change — the 'learning' is transient in-context guidance discarded after the episode. Code (detector/*.py) confirms: reads one trajectory, writes a JSON critical-error report; no memory DB, no persistence, no state carried across episodes.
per_system.outcome_signal: MIXED / two-part. Detection half (transferable): pure LLM-judge — the AgentDebug prompt (Fig.15) takes only {task_description}, 'TASK RESULT: FAILED', and the per-step error analysis; NO gold trajectory, NO counterfactual rollout. Code: fine_grained_analysis.py feeds the LLM trajectory content + task description + env responses + error definitions; the success flag is stored but 'isn't directly incorporated into LLM prompts for error detection.' Recovery-loop half (oracle): the re-rollout stop-condition Eval(tau)==1 (Alg.1) reads the BENCHMARK environment success signal — code: metadata.get('success', metadata.get('won', False)) — to decide a task IS failed and that a re-roll FIXED it. Caveat: the prose 'Stage 2: Critical Error Detection via Counterfactuals... test whether the rollout would succeed' describes the Brute-Force/Binary-Search BASELINES (Table 1), NOT AgentDebug — AgentDebug beats them 24.3% vs 0.0-0.3% ALL precisely by replacing oracle-counterfactual search with an LLM judge.
per_system.transferable_to_session_host: PARTIAL. The transferable primitive: LLM-judged ROOT-CAUSE-STEP attribution over a failed trajectory — an oracle-free way to answer 'WHICH retrieved memory / decision caused the bad outcome' (credit assignment), the exact gap between cmk's 'outcome was bad' and 'down-rank THIS specific memory.' Detection is oracle-free (trajectory + task-desc + 'FAILED' flag -> LLM). NOT directly transferable: (a) AgentDebug's failure TRIGGER and recovery stop-condition both use the benchmark environment success oracle (Eval==1, success/won metadata) — a session host has none; cmk would have to supply an oracle-free failure trigger (user-correction, tool-error, recall-miss — already in portfolio) to fire the attribution; (b) the 'learning' is within-episode in-context only, whereas cmk needs a persistent trust/utility update across sessions, which AgentDebug does NOT do. Directly useful to cmk: the Memory taxonomy (false recall / omission / retrieval failure) as a label set for WHY a retrieved memory was bad, and the finding that Memory is the single most common failure module (38/... in AgentErrorBench, Hallucination 13 + Retrieval Failure 4).
per_system.novel_signal: none new (as a signal TYPE). Closest-to-novel is a MECHANISM not a signal: oracle-free LLM-judged root-cause-STEP attribution / credit-assignment over a failed trajectory (localize WHICH step/memory doomed the outcome), distinct from the portfolio's outcome-level signals which mark THAT something failed but not WHICH memory to blame. Flagging it as 'blame-attribution / credit-assignment' — a per-memory localizer that could ride on top of an existing oracle-free trigger, rather than a new failure detector itself.
per_system.quote: "Stage 2: Critical Error Detection via LLM (no rollout/counterfactuals)" (Algorithm 1) — and the AgentDebug prompt (Fig.15): "You are an expert at identifying critical failure points in agent trajectories... TASK: {task_description}\nTASK RESULT: FAILED\n... Your job is to identify the CRITICAL ERROR - the earliest and most important error that led to task failure... You must take a HOLISTIC, GLOBAL perspective to identify the true root cause of failure. Do NOT rely on any predetermined severity weights or rankings." Recovery-loop oracle (Algorithm 1): "tau(k) <- ReRollout(tau(k-1), t*, phi(k-1)) // Re-execute from t* with feedback ... if Eval(tau(k)) == 1 then return tau(k)". Memory taxonomy: "Memory Errors in recalling or retrieving information (false recall, omission, retrieval failure) that distort subsequent reasoning."

per_system.system: Memento (arXiv 2508.16153, aka AgentFly; repo Memento-Teams/Memento). Memory-augmented online RL over a Memory-augmented MDP (M-MDP): a growing Case Bank of past task trajectories + a case-selection policy (Q-function). Two shipped variants: non-parametric (cosine retrieval over cases tagged reward=0/1) and parametric (a trained SimCSE-based binary classifier retriever, Q ≈ softmax(logits)[:,1]). No fine-tuning of the base LLM; the retriever/case-bank is the only thing that learns.
per_system.failure_mechanism: Every solved query is written to the Case Bank labeled by outcome — no_parametric_cbr.py: `reward = 1 if judge_res["judgement"] == "correct" else 0`, then appended to memory.jsonl; parametric_memory_cbr.py: `case_label = "positive" if is_correct else "negative"` via save_memory_entry. Failed cases are NOT discarded: build_prompt_from_cases injects them as explicit "Negative Examples (reward=0)" to steer the planner AWAY (negative-as-first-class, retrievable contrastive exemplar). On failure the parametric path also calls save_training_data(q, retrieved_cases, is_correct=False) -> writes `truth_label: false`, retraining the retriever (MemoryRetrieverClassifier, CrossEntropyLoss on truth_label) to down-weight that case->query match at next retrieval. So a retrieved memory that led to a bad outcome updates BOTH the case label (down to negative/reward=0) AND the learned retrieval ranking. This is a real utility/ranking update driven by failure, not passive store-and-retrieve — refines wave-1's read rather than repeating it: the failure loop is concrete in code, not just paper.
per_system.outcome_signal: LLM-judge verdict correct/incorrect (`llm_judge`) — but its input is a per-question GOLD ANSWER list loaded from ground_truth_map (`data.get("ground_truth")`). So the outcome signal = benchmark gold-answer comparison, adjudicated by an LLM.
per_system.transferable_to_session_host: Partially / not as-shipped. The reward is gold-answer-gated: both clients require a ground_truth_map keyed by question, and reward/case_label is derived solely from comparing pred_answer to that gold list (`is_correct = judge_res["judgement"] == "correct"`, judge fed `gt_answer`). A session host has NO gold answer at conversation time, so the shipped loop cannot run. HOWEVER the architecture is oracle-agnostic: `reward` is just a scalar/label written to the case; nothing structurally requires it to come from a gold label. Swap the gold-comparison for an oracle-free signal (user-correction, tool-result pass/fail, self-consistency, LLM-judge-without-gold) and the identical case-bank + contrastive-injection + retriever-retrain loop works unchanged. The transferable PATTERNS for cmk: (1) negative-case-as-retrievable-exemplar (keep failed items, inject them labeled as what-NOT-to-do, not just prune/down-rank) — cmk today only dampens trust_score, it never surfaces a contradicted memory as a labeled negative; (2) truth_label retraining of the retriever is the parametric analogue of cmk's deferred re-curation. But the DISCRIMINATOR verdict stands: as implemented Memento needs a benchmark oracle — NOT oracle-free.
per_system.novel_signal: negative-case-as-retrievable-exemplar — failed trajectories are RETAINED and re-injected into the planner prompt as labeled "Negative Examples (reward=0)" contrastive guidance, not merely pruned or down-ranked. Distinct from outdated-as-signal (Memoria deprioritizes/drops) and from rejected-edit-buffer (stores declined edits): here an executed-and-FAILED task is kept and shown as a steer-away exemplar. Everything else maps to the known portfolio (contradiction/supersession already covered; the truth_label retrain is a parametric used-vs-ignored + counterfactual-ish signal but gold-gated).
per_system.quote: no_parametric_cbr.py L564-565: `judge_res = await llm_judge(q, gt, pred_answer)` / `reward = 1 if judge_res["judgement"] == "correct" else 0`; and the judge prompt L38: "You will be given a question and its ground truth answer list ... you need to judge if the pred_answer correctly answers the question based on the ground truth answer list." README L59: "Memento logs successful & failed trajectories into a Case Bank and retrieves by value."

synthesis.complete_denominator:
EXAMINED ACROSS ALL WAVES = 18 (wave-1) + 9 (wave-2) + 29 (triaged-passive) = 56 systems total.

LEARN-FROM-FAILURE TALLY (the "does anything update on a bad retrieved memory?" question):
- Wave-1: 14 yes / 2 partial / 2 no.
- Wave-2 (9): 5 yes (ReMem, EvoTest, SkillRL, SkillAdaptor, Memento) / 4 partial (MUSE, SkillRevise, Negative-Knowledge, AgentDebug) / 0 no.
- Passive-triage (29): count as no.
- COMBINED: 19 yes / 4 partial / 33 no (of 56).

THE LOAD-BEARING NUMBER — ORACLE-FREE systems that CLOSE the learn-from-failure loop with a signal a session host actually has at conversation time (no benchmark reward / unit-test / gold label / env final-state):
- Wave-1: ~4 (memclaw, Memoria, A-MemGuard, SkillOpt-Sleep).
- Wave-2: 0 new. Every wave-2 system that learns from failure at the retrieval/utility layer is benchmark-oracle-gated (ReMem f_t = "correctness signal" from gold labels; EvoTest UCB = Jericho game score; SkillRL r∈{0,1} env reward; SkillAdaptor entry+qualifier gates = benchmark indicator y + re-exec metric m; Memento reward = gold-answer LLM-judge). MUSE is oracle-FREE on its write-gate (LLM-judge completion check) but is PASSIVE on the retrieval-failure loop — zero utility/trust/count field, no down-rank/prune on a retrieved-SOP-caused failure — so it does not close the loop.
- STILL ~4 ORACLE-FREE LOOP-CLOSERS across all 56 examined. This is the number that matters, and wave-2 did not move it.
new_novel_signals: weighted-per-memory-blame-attribution (SkillAdaptor Linker + AgentDebug root-cause-step + MUSE's absence-by-contrast): an oracle-FREE, single-trajectory LLM-judge credit-assignment that splits blame ACROSS the recalled set — 'which of the injected memories caused this bad turn' as a weighted distribution, not the portfolio's per-fact binary. This is a MECHANISM that rides on top of an existing oracle-free trigger (user-correction/tool-error/contradiction), not a new failure DETECTOR. It fills cmk's real gap: cmk today flat-dampens the whole recalled set; this localizes the culprit. NEW to the portfolio.

route-closure / explicit-dead-end veto (Negative-Knowledge recommended_action=abandon_route + ruled_out_routes + rejects_bank): a memory that functions as a NEGATIVE CONSTRAINT the reader must reject-AGAINST — a first-class 'do-not-retry-this-route' marker, distinct from contradiction (two facts disagreeing). WRITE-TIME content signal, oracle-free to write; does NOT close the ranking-layer loop.

negative-case-as-retrievable-exemplar (Memento 'Negative Examples (reward=0)' contrastive injection): retain a failed item and re-surface it labeled as what-NOT-to-do, rather than only prune/down-rank it. cmk today only dampens trust_score and never surfaces a contradicted memory as a labeled negative steer. Architecture is oracle-agnostic; only Memento's LABEL is gold-gated.

severity-weighted-failure-sampling (SkillRL): prioritize learning by HOW-BAD the failure was, not just whether it failed — oracle-derived there (severity = reward magnitude), so NOT oracle-free, but a signal-SHAPING idea if cmk ever gets a graded transferable outcome (e.g. same fact re-corrected twice = higher severity).

typed-failure-taxonomy as memory PACKAGING (Negative-Knowledge 6-way layer/scope/degree/action/risk vocabulary): elevates Memoria's 'outdated-as-first-class' into a structured negative-fact schema. A packaging idea, not a new detector.
synthesis.verdict_change:
NO OVERTURN — wave-2 CONFIRMS the wave-1 picture on every load-bearing point, and hardens it.

(1) Oracle-free loop-closing is RARE and did not grow: 9 new systems, 0 new oracle-free loop-closers. The count stays ~4 (memclaw, Memoria, A-MemGuard, SkillOpt-Sleep). If anything wave-2 STRENGTHENS the wave-1 read that the field's learn-from-failure machinery is overwhelmingly benchmark-oracle-gated — 5 of 9 wave-2 systems learn from failure, ALL 5 via a ground-truth reward (gold label / game score / env success / re-execution metric). The "~12 need a benchmark oracle" bucket just grew by 5.

(2) The 'inert utility field' anti-pattern is CONFIRMED field-wide and MUSE extends it into self-evolving agents: MUSE, Negative-Knowledge, and SkillRevise instantiate it (MUSE = no utility field at all + success-gated writes; Negative-Knowledge = no utility/trust/count field, whole bank injected inline; SkillRevise = utility used only as a failed-candidate tie-breaker). ReMem is the interesting near-miss: it AVOIDS the inert field by having no field at all and pushing the judgment into an LLM Refine pass — but gates that pass on the oracle-derived f_t, so it's not an oracle-free escape.

(3) Memoria stays the cleanest oracle-free loop-closer. Memento is Memoria's parametric cousin but gold-gated as shipped (reward = gold-answer LLM-judge), so it does NOT dislodge Memoria — it corroborates that the ARCHITECTURE is oracle-agnostic while the shipped SIGNAL is not.

(4) Portfolio is NOT yet saturated on signal TYPES — wave-2 added a genuinely new one: weighted-per-memory-blame-attribution (oracle-free credit-assignment across the recalled set). The wave-1 portfolio marked THAT-something-failed; it lacked WHICH-recalled-memory-to-blame. Two converging wave-2 systems (SkillAdaptor Linker, AgentDebug root-cause-step) supply an oracle-free mechanism for exactly that. So: portfolio saturated on oracle-free DETECTORS (no new failure triggers), NOT saturated on oracle-free ATTRIBUTION mechanisms.
synthesis.updated_recommendation:
Denominator now 56 examined, ~4 oracle-free loop-closers. The wave-1 recommendation holds and gets ONE concrete addition.

KEEP (wave-1, confirmed): model cmk's trust_score loop on Memoria — self-reported {useful/irrelevant/outdated/wrong} down-ranks the memory at next retrieval via a bounded multiplier — because it is the cleanest of the only ~4 oracle-free loop-closers and it is a RANKING signal, exactly what cmk wants (not an injected lesson like A-MemGuard, not a fallback tier like SkillOpt). Wave-2 supplies no better template; every wave-2 loop-closer needs an oracle cmk lacks.

ADD (wave-2's real contribution — the blame-attribution layer): cmk's current design flat-dampens the WHOLE recalled/injected set when an outcome is bad. That is the gap wave-2 exposes. Adopt the SkillAdaptor-Linker / AgentDebug root-cause mechanism — an oracle-free LLM-judge that, when an existing oracle-free trigger fires (user-correction, tool-error, contradiction — all already in cmk's portfolio), reads the turn and produces a WEIGHTED responsibility distribution over the injected memories, then down-weights/rewrites ONLY the culprit's trust_score instead of dampening all. This is single-trajectory, needs no re-execution, and is the one new oracle-free capability wave-2 offers. CRITICAL DISCARD: do NOT copy SkillAdaptor's Qualification gate (accept-edit-only-if-re-execution-scores-higher) or Memento's gold-answer reward or ReMem's f_t — those are the oracle-gated halves that make the published numbers hold and that cmk structurally cannot build at conversation time. Harvest the attribution, discard the acceptance-check.

SECONDARY (lower priority, oracle-free, borrow the SHAPE not the signal): (a) negative-case-as-retrievable-exemplar (Memento) — when cmk contradicts/supersedes a fact, consider surfacing the old fact labeled as a what-NOT-to-do steer rather than only dampening it silently; (b) route-closure veto + typed-failure taxonomy (Negative-Knowledge) — a first-class 'dead-end / do-not-retry' negative-fact type with a structured {degree, action, risk} schema, richer than a free-prose cautionary bullet; (c) the failure-lesson SHAPE from SkillRL {point-of-failure, flawed-action, what-should-have-been-done, general-principle} as the structured content of a cautionary memory emitted from a user-correction. All three are WRITE-TIME/packaging improvements, oracle-free to write; none closes the ranking loop by itself — the ranking loop stays Memoria + the new blame-attribution layer.
synthesis.honesty_note:
Evidence quality varies and I am not smoothing it: 4 of 9 wave-2 findings are CODE-verified (MUSE, Negative-Knowledge, AgentDebug, Memento), 5 are PAPER-only (ReMem, EvoTest, SkillRL, SkillRevise, SkillAdaptor — SkillAdaptor's repo is promised-but-unreleased, so its 'oracle-free Linker' claim is paper-asserted and its standalone reliability is UNPROVEN outside the benchmark harness it's yoked to). Treat the blame-attribution recommendation as pattern-transfer from two convergent PAPER descriptions (SkillAdaptor + AgentDebug, the latter code-confirmed for detection), not from a shipped oracle-free implementation — no one has demonstrated weighted per-memory blame-attribution running WITHOUT a benchmark oracle end-to-end; cmk would be the first to try it on user-correction/tool-error triggers.

Gaps: (1) I did not independently re-verify any arXiv id, quote, or code path — I am synthesizing the provided deep-findings JSON at face value; the verbatim quotes are as-supplied. (2) The '~4 oracle-free loop-closers' and '56 examined' counts inherit wave-1's classifications, which I did not re-audit; if a wave-1 'yes' was mis-scored, the denominator shifts. (3) The 29 'triaged-passive' are counted as 'no' without re-reading — a coarse bucket. (4) MUSE's classification as 'partial' is a judgment call: it's oracle-FREE on writes but PASSIVE on the retrieval loop; I counted it partial (learns-by-accumulation) not no, but it contributes ZERO to the oracle-free-loop-closer count, which is the number that actually drives the recommendation. (5) The key unproven assumption in the recommendation: that an oracle-free LLM-judge can reliably attribute per-memory blame across a recalled set at conversation time. Every code-verified instance of that mechanism (AgentDebug) ran within-episode only and its recovery loop still touched a benchmark success flag; the fully-oracle-free version is a design proposal, not an observed result.
